original: Roman Senate declares Julius Caesar an enemy of the Roman Republic
Pope Stefanus II arrives in Ponthion
Afonso IV succeeds Denis as King of Portugal
Calais, last English possession in France, retaken by France
Last day of the Julian calendar in Bohemia and The Holy Roman Empire
Boris Godunov seizes Russian throne on death of Feodor I
Fire destroys Jamestown, Virginia
Germany & Transylvania sign Peace of Nikolsburg
Fire after heavy storm destroys two thirds of De Rijp, Netherlands, 1 person dies
Typewriter patented by Englishman Henry Mill (built years later)
1st US commercial bank, Bank of North America, opens in Philadelphia
1st US seed business established by David Landreth, Philadelphia
1st balloon flight across English Channel by Jean Pierre Blanchard and John Jeffries
French Revolution: A major riot breaks out in Versailles as people demand lower bread prices
First official use of the modern Italian flag (the Tricolor) by the Cispadane Republic
2nd Bank of US opens in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
1st printing in Hawaii of a ceremonial broadside for King Kamehameha II by Elisha Loomis
Free American Blacks establish a settlement on Providence Island, off the coast of West Africa; later becomes the city of Monrovia, Liberia
Liberia colonized by the American Colonization Society
1st US Railroad Station opens in Baltimore
HMS Beagle anchors off Chonos Archipelago
Florida troops takeover Fort Marion at St Augustine (US Civil War)
Arkansas constitutional convention meets in Little Rock
Mississippi constitutional convention meets in Jackson
African American inventor William Purvis receives a patent for the fountain pen
Mine explosion kills 100 in Krebs, Oklahoma; blacks trying to help rescue white survivors, driven away with guns
Hermann Sudermanns' play "Heimat" premieres in Berlin
Fannie Farmer publishes her first cookbook "The Boston Cooking-School Cook Book"
Walter Camp publishes his 1st All-American football team in Collier's
England beat Australia by one wicket at the MCG
Stanley Cup, Dey's Arena, Ottawa, ON: Ottawa Senators beat Galt (ON), 3-1 for a 2-0 sweep of challenge series
Dutch Scouts Organization established in Amsterdam
William M. Burton patents a process to "crack" petroleum
World War I: Germany's Kaiser Wilhelm approves strategic bombing of Britain, but forbids bombing London, fearing his relatives in the royal family might be killed
Montenegrin guerrilla fighters rebel, but fail to prevent Serbia's annexation of Montenegro
The Industrial and Commercial Workers' Union of South Africa is founded, led by Clements Kadalie
Five duly elected Socialist assemblymen are denied by the New York State Assembly
Baltimore Sun warns of the Ku Klux Klan
Commercial transatlantic telephone service inaugurated between New York & London
Harlem Globetrotters play their 1st game in Hinckley, Illinois
"Buck Rogers" sci-fi comic strip's 1st appearance in a newspaper

French inventor Robert Bureau launches the 1st radiosonde - a battery-powered telemetry instrument carried into the atmosphere to measure various parameters and transmit them by radio to a ground receiver
Edwin Justus Mayer's "Children of Darkness" premieres in NYC
French physicist Marguerite Perey discovers francium (Fr), the last naturally occurring element to be found
Guy Menzies flies the first solo non-stop trans-Tasman flight (from Australia to New Zealand) in 11 hours and 45 minutes, crash-landing on New Zealand's west coast.
1st game played at Orchard Lake Curling Club, Mich
1st edition of People & Fatherland published in Netherlands
"Flash Gordon" comic strip created and drawn by Alex Raymond debuts
Zoe Akins' "Old Maid" premieres in NYC
Tennis champs Helen Moody & Howard Kinsley volley 2,001 times (1h18m)
US labor union leader Thomas Mooney freed from prison and later pardoned based on perjured testimony; he had been jailed since 1916 on charges related to an anarchist bombing in San Francisco
Winter War: The Finish 9th Division defeats the Soviet forces on the Raate-Suomussalmi road despite being significantly outnumbered
WWII siege of Bataan in the Philippines starts (Americans and Filippino forces surrender in April)
US Air Force announces production of 1st US jet fighter, the Bell P-59
The last surface engagement between Allies and Japanese in the Pacific campaign, World War II
Cambodia becomes autonomous state inside French Union
Australia v England at MCG drawn in 6 days, 1st cricket draw in Australia since 1882
Thomas Mantell, a pilot for the Kentucky Air National Guard, crashes while pursuing a supposed UFO
1st photo of genes taken at University of Southern California by Pease & Baker
Hank Snow's 1st appearance on "Grand Ole Opry"
Mental health wing of Mercy Hospital burns, kills 41 in Davenport, Iowa
US PresidentHarry Trumanannounces American development of the hydrogen bomb
Georgetown-IBM experiment, the first public demonstration of a machine translation system, is held at IBM's headquarters in New York
WCIQ TV channel 7 in Mt Cheaha, AL (PBS) begins broadcasting
Indian batsman Vinoo Mankad scores 231 v NZ, 413 opening stand with Roy
Algerian militant and National Liberation Front member Djamila Bouhired sets off a bomb in an Algiers cafe killing 11 civilians, precipitating the Battle of Algiers
USSR reduces army to 300,000
1st NFL Playoff Bowl (runner-up bowl)-Detroit beats Cleveland 17-16
Trucial States (now UAE) issue their 1st postage stamps
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to John Hall Wheelock
1st class postage raised from 4 cents to 5 cents
Bahamas becomes self-governing
Dick Weber rolls highest bowling game in air (Boeing 707)
France announces it will convert $150 million of its currency to gold
Twin brothers are in held in custody in London charged with abduction and murder
"GE College Bowl" quiz show premieres on NBC TV
1st class US postage raised from 5 cents to 6 cents
Surveyor 7, the last spacecraft in the Surveyor series, lifts off
Dance Theatre of Harlem ballet school opens in a church basement
US Congress doubles presidential salary
-40°F (-40°C) in Hawley Lake, Arizona (state record)
Discovery of the first black hole Cygnus X-1 in the constellation Cygnus reported in a paper in "Nature" by Louise Webster and Paul Murdin
Iberian Airlines 'plane crashes into 800ft peak on island of Ibiza, 104 die
LA Lakers defeat the Atlanta Hawks 134-90 for their 33rd straight win, the longest winning streak in major professional sports
William Hubbs Rehnquist sworn in as US Supreme Court Justice
American poet James Merrill wins Bollingen Prize
British Darts Organisation founded in North London
Johnny Watkins bowls six overs 0-21 v Pakistan Never again
Mark Essex's mass shooting comes to an end after he is shot by police more than 200 times on the roof of New Orlean's Holiday Inn hotel. He killed nine people, including five policeman.
WNPB TV channel 13 in Marquette, MI (PBS) begins broadcasting
Gasoline rationing begins in the Netherlands
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to AR Ammons (Sphere)
Led Zeppelin fans riot before Boston concert causing $30,000 damage
Angola revises its constitution in include a state security law
Vietnamese forces capture Phnom Penh from Khmer Rouge
Minnesota North Stars end Philadelphia Flyers' NHL record 35 game unbeaten streak with a 7-1 win at the Met Center in Bloomington, Minnesota
TV series "Fame", based on the film of the same name, starring Debbie Allen and an ensemble cast premieres on NBC TV
Brunei becomes the sixth member of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).
Japanese space probe Sakigake launched to Halley's comet
KHQ-AM in Spokane, Washington, changes call letters to KLSN (now KAQQ)
Netherlands Bank issues 250 guilder notes
STS 61-C mission scrubbed at T -9m because of weather problems
USPresident Reaganannounces economic sanctions against Libya
French airplanes harass Libyan positions in Duadi Doum
Cleveland Cavaliers block 21 NY Knicks shots tying NBA regulation game record
International Conference on Limitation of Chemical Weapons opens in Paris
NBA's Cleveland Cavaliers then longest winning streak (11 games)
Lynn Jennings runs world record indoor 5km indoor at 15:22.64
Tower of Pisa closed to the public after leaning too far
"Nia Peeples Party Machine" premieres on TV
AT&T releases video-telephone ($1,499)
Bosnian War: Bosnian Army launches a surprise attack on Kravica, a village in Srebrenica
The Fourth Republic of Ghana is inaugurated with Jerry Rawlings as President
South Africa beat Australia in the Sydney Test by 5 runs
United Express commuter plane crashes in Ohio, killing 5
Larry Brown posts his 500th career NBA coaching victory, following the Indiana Pacers' 88-83 road win at the Houston Rockets
16th United Negro College Fund raises $12,600,000
NBA Board of Governors unanimously ratifies a new 6-year collective bargaining agreement between the league and the National Basketball Players Association
Former UK Cabinet minister Jonathan Aitken released from prison after 9 months of a 18-month sentence
Danish TV drama "The Killing" created by Søren Sveistrup and starring Sofie Gråbøl premieres on DR1
Muslim gunmen in Egypt kill nine people after opening fire on a crowd of Coptic Christians
Hot air balloon crashes in Carterton, New Zealand, killing 11
A car bomb explodes in Sana'a, the capital of Yemen, killing 38 people and injuring more than 63
Snow falls in the Sahara Desert, with 15 inches reported in Aïn Séfra, northwestern Algeria
Sydney, Australia has its hottest day for 80 years as Penrith reaches 47.3°C degrees
Amazon overtakes Microsoft to become the world's most valuable listed company for the first time, worth $797 billion
Attempted coup in Gabon fails after rebel soldiers overtake national radio station in Libreville
Rain ruins last 2 days of 4th cricket Test in Sydney as India wins series 2-1; become first Asian side to win a Test series in Australia, 71 years after the first Indian touring team in 1947-48
Tennessee woman Cyntoia Brown, convicted for murdering a man aged 16, granted clemency by Governor Bill Haslam
6.4 magnitude earthquake in Puerto Rico, island's largest in a century, followed by many aftershocks kill 1 person and destroy 800 homes
Arizona called the COVID-19 "hotspot of the world" by local health officials as state suffers an average of 118.3 new cases per 100,000 people
WHO warns Europe needs to do more to "flatten the steep vertical line" of COVID-19 cases and control the spread of the new variant, with 230 million already living under lockdown
First successful transplant of a pig's heart into a human when genetically modified pig's heart inserted into a 53-year-old man in Baltimore, Maryland
Three men convicted of murdering black jogger Ahmaud Arbery in Feb 2020, sentenced to life in prison in a Georgia court
Vaccine for bees against American Foulbrood bacteria approved in the US - first vaccine in the US for any insect
Bangladesh's Prime MinisterSheikh Hasinawins her fourth term in office amid controversial election marred by opposition boycotts with over 10,000 activists arrested
7.1-magnitude earthquake strikes Tibet, near the holy city of Shigatse, also causing damage in Nepal, China and India, killing about 126 people
Extreme Santa Ana winds cause wildfires across Los Angeles, including the Palisades, Eaton and Hurst fires causing tens of thousands to evacuate, burning over 16,000 structures and killing 29 people

The body of former US PresidentJimmy Carterlies in state in the US Capitol
Antonio "Michele" Ghislieri is elected PopePius V

Trial of Hungarian aristocratElizabeth Báthoryfor killing and torturing hundreds of young women, later sentenced to house arrest for the rest of her life
Francis Baconbecomes Lord Chancellor of England
Russian TsarPeter the Greatdeparts Netherlands for England
Gioachino Rossini's cantata "Stabat Mater" premieres at the Théâtre-Italien's Salle Ventadour in Paris, France
Romney Campaign:Stonewall Jacksonmarch towards Romney, West Virginia
William K.L. Dickson captures "Fred Ott's Sneeze" as a motion picture atThomas Edison's Black Maria Studio in West Orange, New Jersey
Teatro Amazonas in Manaus, Brazil, opens with a performance by Italian tenorEnrico Caruso
Vincent d'Indy's opera "L'etranger" premieres in Brussels
MarconiCo. establishes "CQD" (Seeking You. Distress!) as 1st international radio distress signal - not adopted universally
Clyde Fitch's play "The Truth" premieres in NYC

"Tarzan", one of the 1st adventure comic strips, 1st appears
Croatian nationalist and fascist movement,Ustašafounded byAnte Pavelićin exile in Italy

Marian Andersonis the first African American to perform with the New York Metropolitan Opera, singing the role of Ulrica inGiuseppe Verdi's "Un ballo in maschera"

Gibson Guitars receives US patent for their 'Flying V' electric guitar model; eventually used by Albert Collins,Jimi Hendrix, Marc Bolan of T-Rex, Dave Davies of the Kinks, and Billy Gibbons of ZZ Top
American gangsterMeyer Lanskyflees Cuba for the Bahamas due to the Cuban Revolution and rise of Fidel Castro
US recognizesFidel Castro's Cuban government

The rift between Cleveland Browns ownerArt Modelland head coachPaul Brownreaches a boiling point, and Brown is fired
Gene Kiniski beatsLou Theszin St Louis, to become NWA wrestling champion
Lewis F. Powell Jr.becomes a US Supreme Court Justice
Real Records releases "Pretenders" in the UK, the debut album byThe Pretenders

Saddam Husseinprepares his troops for what he says will be a long violent war against the US

Newt Gingrichnarrowly re-elected speaker of the US House of Representatives

"In Da Club" single is released by50 Cent(MTV Video Music Award Best Rap Video and Best New Artist 2003, Billboard Song of the Year 2003)
Phil Jacksonwins his 900th game as a head coach, becoming the fastest coach to reach 900 career wins
"The King's Speech", starringColin Firth,Geoffrey Rush, andHelena Bonham Carter, is released in the UK

Elon Musk, co-founder of Tesla, becomes the world's richest person, worth $186 billion, overtaking Amazon founderJeff Bezos
